title: Map treble response to fine detail
summary: Add EMA smoothing to treble energy in the render loop, then enhance the particle field system with three distinct treble-driven detail effects (brightness modulation, size shimmer, and positional jitter) that are visibly different from the bass macro-motion mapping. The existing trebleToShimmer mapping and computeTrebleAvg extraction are already in place — this ticket wires smoothed treble into richer visual effects and adds tests to verify acceptance criteria.

steps.title: Add EMA smoothing utility for treble energy in renderLoop.ts
steps.description: Introduce an exponential moving average (EMA) to smooth raw treble values frame-to-frame, preventing flicker. Add a `smoothTreble` variable scoped inside `startLoop` that blends the new treble value each frame: `smoothed = smoothed * alpha + raw * (1 - alpha)` with alpha ~0.85. Pass the smoothed value into `mapSignalsToVisuals` instead of the raw treble average.

details.approach: Add a `let smoothTreble = 0` closure variable. After `computeTrebleAvg`, update: `smoothTreble = smoothTreble * 0.85 + treble * 0.15`. Pass `smoothTreble` as the `treble` input to `mapSignalsToVisuals`. This uses the Web Audio API's own `smoothingTimeConstant=0.8` as a first stage, plus this EMA as a second stage for organic response.
details.rationale: AC2 requires smoothed response to avoid flicker. Two-stage smoothing (Web Audio + application EMA) ensures treble detail feels organic rather than noisy.

tests.description: Treble energy is smoothed across consecutive frames — a spike followed by silence decays gradually rather than dropping to zero instantly
Smoothed treble value stays within 0-1 range after mapping

steps.title: Enhance particle field with distinct treble-driven detail effects
steps.description: Expand the existing treble application in particleField.ts beyond the current basic size/brightness modulation. Add three perceptible detail effects driven by trebleEnergy: (1) brightness/lightness pulsing (already exists, refine range), (2) size shimmer with per-particle phase variation for sparkle, (3) positional micro-jitter perpendicular to velocity for fine-grained texture. These effects must be visibly distinct from bass's macro motion (large displacement amplification).

changes.approach: Use `(Math.sin(frame.time * 0.005 + p.hueOffset) + 1) * 0.5` as a per-particle phase offset, multiply by `trebleEnergy` to create staggered size pulsing. This creates a sparkle effect where particles shimmer at different phases.

Add small perpendicular displacement: `jitterX = Math.sin(frame.time * 0.01 + i) * trebleEnergy * 0.003` applied to render position (not stored position) so it doesn't accumulate. This is fundamentally different from bass which amplifies velocity — treble adds high-frequency positional noise.
Increase lightness range from `50 + trebleEnergy * 20` to `50 + trebleEnergy * 30` and add per-particle phase variation for a more alive feel.
Switch from `hsl()` to `hsla()` with alpha = `0.6 + trebleEnergy * 0.4`, so particles become more opaque and vivid on treble hits. This adds another perceptible channel distinct from bass.

details.key_distinction_from_bass: Bass drives macro motion (velocity amplification via `bassBoost`). Treble drives micro detail (size sparkle, positional jitter, brightness/alpha variation). Bass moves particles farther; treble makes them shimmer in place.

tests.description: Treble energy influences at least one visual detail property — particles rendered with high trebleEnergy produce different fillStyle/size than with trebleEnergy=0

Treble effect is visibly distinct from bass — with only treble active (bass=0), particles change appearance but not macro displacement; with only bass active (treble=0), particles displace but don't shimmer
Scene remains readable — particle positions stay within canvas bounds even with maximum treble energy
Treble jitter does not accumulate in stored particle positions — positions after N frames with treble are bounded similarly to frames without treble

steps.title: Verify acceptance criteria and definition of done
steps.description: Run full test suite, verify build passes, manually confirm no console errors. Ensure all four acceptance criteria are met: (AC1) treble influences detail, (AC2) smoothing prevents flicker, (AC3) effect distinct from bass, (AC4) scene readable.
verification: npm run build — passes with no errors
npm run test — all existing + new tests pass
No forbidden storage APIs (cookies, localStorage) used in new code
Scene renders correctly with and without audio autoplay

files_to_modify.changes: Add EMA smoothing for treble energy (closure variable + blend each frame)
Enhance draw() with per-particle sparkle shimmer, micro-jitter on render position, brightness range increase, alpha/opacity variation
Add T-019-01, T-019-02 for treble smoothing verification
Add T-019-03 through T-019-06 for treble detail effects

risks.mitigation: Alpha 0.85 is a starting point — can tune down to 0.7 if treble feels sluggish. Web Audio's own smoothingTimeConstant=0.8 already provides a first pass.

risks.risk: Jitter makes scene feel noisy rather than detailed
risks.mitigation: Jitter is applied to render position only (not stored position) and scaled by trebleEnergy, so it's zero when there's no treble. Magnitude (0.003) is small relative to canvas size.
Canvas 2D handles hsla efficiently. No measurable overhead expected for particle counts ≤600.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.AC1_treble_influences_detail: Step 2 — shimmer, jitter, brightness, alpha all driven by trebleEnergy. Tested by T-019-03.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.AC2_smoothed_no_flicker: Step 1 — EMA smoothing in renderLoop.ts. Tested by T-019-01.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.AC3_distinct_from_bass: Step 2 — bass amplifies velocity (macro motion), treble modulates size/brightness/jitter (micro detail). Tested by T-019-04.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.AC4_scene_readable: Step 2 — jitter on render position only, bounded magnitude, positions stay in canvas. Tested by T-019-05, T-019-06.
